model_summary: Initial gVAR-ready observation model targeting nighttime cognitive arousal and its downstream morning reactivity. Five criteria (C01–C05) capture nocturnal replay, anticipatory worry, global nighttime arousal, delayed sleep onset, and morning irritability. Five modifiable predictors (P01–P05) span circadian entrainment, evening light exposure, presleep cognitive offloading, presleep worry-reduction routines, and a structured morning routine. The plan emphasizes within-person variance via brief EMAs and lightweight passive sensors, with a compact predictor–criterion grid to seed gVAR estimation while avoiding redundancy and collinearity.
variable_selection_notes: Chosen variables balance coverage of the complaint’s core mechanisms (nocturnal cognitive arousal and delayed sleep onset) with actionable levers. Predictors emphasize circadian regulation (P01, P02, P05) and cognitive-behavioral strategies at bedtime (P03, P04). We avoid duplicative self-regulation constructs by selecting one broad circadian optimization construct (P01/P02/P05) and two presleep cognitive strategies (P03/P04). Within-person variance is supported by 4 momentary EMAs/day plus a daily diary, and by passive signals for light exposure and screen-use. Transformations planned (z-scoring per-person) to address nonstationarity."

momentary_ema.notes: Prompts cover evening/nighttime windows to capture C01–C03, with one block in early morning to profile C05 via a morning diary.

daily_diary.notes: End-of-night sleep diary and morning mood diary to contextualize C04 and C05.

passive_streams.notes: Ambient light and screen usage/passive blue-light exposure data to complement EMAs. Data gaps flagged for follow-up.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Feasible with smartphone sensors and wearables; moderate missingness risk mitigated by reminders and passive data fusion.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: 4 daily EMAs + 1 daily diary yields substantial within-person variance for C01–C05; passive signals add additional variance in P01–P05.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Potential gaps due to device noncompliance; plan includes reminders and fallback to self-report when passive data unavailable.

safety_notes: Not a diagnosis. No medication dosing or treatment recommendations. If distress or harm risk emerges, provide safety guidance to seek professional help.

criteria_variables.label: Nighttime replay of past conversations

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 30 minutes, rate the extent of involuntary replay of past conversations during evening/night (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely).
measurement.notes: Examples: replaying conversations; difficulty shifting attention.

predictor_variables.label: Morning daylight exposure routine
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Morning light resets circadian phase, increases morning alertness, and reduces nocturnal arousal by stabilizing the sleep–wake system.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Outdoor morning walk; 10–20 minutes of daylight exposure

measurement.item_or_signal: Morning daylight exposure minutes within 0-2 hours after waking
measurement.notes: Aggregate daily exposure; aligns with wake-time.

expected_effects.rationale: Less nocturnal replay expected with stronger morning light signaling stable circadian rhythm.
Reduced anticipatory worry aligned with stabilized wake cycle.
Lower nocturnal arousal when circadian entrainment improves.
Earlier/more stable sleep onset via daytime circadian cues.
Smoothed morning irritability through improved circadian alignment.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: No sensitive data; ensure privacy of sensor data and consent for wearables.

feasibility.notes: Passive light data is feasible with standard devices; flow to EMAs remains essential for subjective states.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best single match: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / circadian_entrainment / light_cues / morning_bright_light_exposure. The variable label is Morning daylight exposure routine, and the measurement is passive_phone_sensor capturing Morning daylight exposure minutes after waking. This leaf explicitly encapsulates morning light exposure as a circadian entrainment cue (morning_bright_light_exposure), aligning with the mechanism that morning light resets circadian phase and increases morning alertness to stabilize the sleep–wake cycle. It is the most specific leaf among candidates for a daylight exposure routine in the morning, matching both the measurement focus (light-based signal) and the targeted mechanism.

predictor_variables.label: Evening blue-light exposure reduction
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Reducing blue-light exposure in the evening supports melatonin onset and reduces cognitive arousal at night.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Limit screen use after 9 PM; enable blue-light filter; use warmer lighting.

measurement.item_or_signal: Evening screen-time with blue-light exposure minutes
measurement.notes: Captures evening light exposure and device-use habits.

expected_effects.rationale: Evening light reduction may lower nocturnal cognitive arousal.
Less late-night worry due to calmer evenings.
Overall nighttime activation reduced by circadian stabilization.
Improved sleep onset via reduced stimulation.
Less morning irritability through calmer evenings.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Uses passive signals; ensure privacy and opt-in for screen-light data.

feasibility.notes: Practical with most wearables; verify consent for screen-behavior data.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable P02 maps to 'Evening blue-light exposure reduction' and the best-matching predictor leaf is blue_light_reduction_evening (idx 4677). Mechanism rationale matches: reducing evening blue light supports melatonin onset and reduces nocturnal cognitive arousal, improving circadian entrainment and downstream morning reactivity. The pseudoprofile context emphasizes circadian regulation through evening light management, supporting this mapping as the most specific operational match.

predictor_variables.label: Presleep detachment/unloading routine
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Structured detachment reduces perseverative cognition at sleep onset.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Detachment routines (e.g., cognitive diffusion, labeling thoughts) before bed.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 60 minutes, rate how well you detached/unloaded thoughts before sleep (1-9).
measurement.notes: Represents cognitive offloading quality prior to sleep.

expected_effects.rationale: Detachment reduces nocturnal replay by offloading cognitive load.
Less worry propagation into the night when detachment is practiced.
Lower global arousal through cognitive offloading.
Easier sleep initiation via calmer pre-sleep state.
Improved morning mood by reducing pre-sleep arousal.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: No sensitive data; ensure privacy in EMA responses.

feasibility.notes: EMA-based detachment task is feasible with brief prompts.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 30 minutes, rate the extent of involuntary replay of past conversations during evening/night (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely).
measurement.notes: Examples: replaying conversations; difficulty shifting attention.

criteria.label: Nighttime anticipatory worry / list-making
criteria.query_text_used: Nighttime anticipatory worry / list-making | Operational item/signal: In the past 30 minutes, rate the extent of thinking about possible tomorrow negatives and lists (1-9). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Includes mental simulations of negative outcomes for the near future.

mapping.rationale: The variable 'Nighttime anticipatory worry / list-making' targets nocturnal cognitive rumination about tomorrow's negatives and future planning (lists). The measurement item asks for thinking about possible tomorrow negatives and listing them in the past 30 minutes, i.e., nocturnal worried ruminations. The leaf nocturnal_worried_ruminations precisely represents this nocturnal, future-focused worry pattern, making it the most specific operational match among candidates.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 30 minutes, rate the extent of thinking about possible tomorrow negatives and lists (1-9).
measurement.notes: Includes mental simulations of negative outcomes for the near future.

criteria.label: Nighttime cognitive hyperarousal
criteria.query_text_used: Nighttime cognitive hyperarousal | Operational item/signal: In the past 30 minutes, rate overall mental activation/difficulty quieting thoughts (1-9). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Captures global arousal irrespective of content.

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf idx 26349: nocturnal_cognitive_arousal (CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / insomnia disorders / chronic insomnia disorder / sleep-maintenance insomnia / nocturnal_cognitive_arousal). The variable C03 (Nighttime cognitive hyperarousal) corresponds to cognitive arousal at night. The measurement item (In the past 30 minutes, rate overall mental activation/difficulty quieting thoughts) is an EMA self-report on smartphone app with a 1-9 scale, 4 assessments/day, recalling past 30 minutes. This directly operationalizes nocturnal cognitive arousal as a global nighttime arousal construct, providing the most specific operational leaf for this criterion within sleep-wake/insomnia domains per the pseudoprofile context.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 30 minutes, rate overall mental activation/difficulty quieting thoughts (1-9).
measurement.notes: Captures global arousal irrespective of content.

criteria.label: Delayed sleep onset (3am bedtime)
criteria.query_text_used: Delayed sleep onset (3am bedtime) | Operational item/signal: Last night, how difficult was it to fall asleep? (1 = very easy, 9 = extremely difficult). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Proxy for delayed sleep onset; complements objective sleep timing when available.

mapping.rationale: Variable maps to subjective difficulty initiating sleep (delayed sleep onset) as captured by EMA self-report asking 'Last night, how difficult was it to fall asleep?' on a 1–9 scale. The closest leaf is sleep_onset_difficulty under insomnia-related sleep-wake disorders; it directly matches the label C04 and the measurement type (end-of-day self-report) without conflating circadian timing or pediatric-specific delay tactics.

measurement.item_or_signal: Last night, how difficult was it to fall asleep? (1 = very easy, 9 = extremely difficult).
measurement.notes: Proxy for delayed sleep onset; complements objective sleep timing when available.

criteria.label: Morning interpersonal irritability
criteria.query_text_used: Morning interpersonal irritability | Operational item/signal: At approximately 8:00 AM today, rate your irritability toward others on a 1-9 scale. | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Captures morning reactivity to social stimuli.

mapping.rationale: Variable: Morning interpersonal irritability; measurement: morning EMA self-report of irritability toward others at ~8:00 AM on a 1-9 scale. The closest operational leaf is irritable_mood_present, which directly represents the presence of irritable mood. This captures a morning, interpersonal affective state consistent with the variable label and measurement item, whereas more clinical or morning-disturbance leaves describe broader or different psychopathology. Hence, irritable_mood_present is the most specific, mood-state–level match among candidates.

measurement.item_or_signal: At approximately 8:00 AM today, rate your irritability toward others on a 1-9 scale.
measurement.notes: Captures morning reactivity to social stimuli.

predictors.label: Morning daylight exposure routine
predictors.query_text_used: Morning daylight exposure routine | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Morning light resets circadian phase, increases morning alertness, and reduces nocturnal arousal by stabilizing the sleep–wake system. | Intervention examples: Outdoor morning walk; 10–20 minutes of daylight exposure | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | Morning daylight exposure minutes within 0-2 hours after waking | Prior ontology hint: Sleep_circadian_and_restoration / Circadian_Entrainment

mapping.rationale: Best single match: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / circadian_entrainment / light_cues / morning_bright_light_exposure. The variable label is Morning daylight exposure routine, and the measurement is passive_phone_sensor capturing Morning daylight exposure minutes after waking. This leaf explicitly encapsulates morning light exposure as a circadian entrainment cue (morning_bright_light_exposure), aligning with the mechanism that morning light resets circadian phase and increases morning alertness to stabilize the sleep–wake cycle. It is the most specific leaf among candidates for a daylight exposure routine in the morning, matching both the measurement focus (light-based signal) and the targeted mechanism.

source_variable.label: Morning daylight exposure routine
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Morning light resets circadian phase, increases morning alertness, and reduces nocturnal arousal by stabilizing the sleep–wake system.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Outdoor morning walk; 10–20 minutes of daylight exposure

measurement.item_or_signal: Morning daylight exposure minutes within 0-2 hours after waking
measurement.notes: Aggregate daily exposure; aligns with wake-time.

expected_effects.rationale: Less nocturnal replay expected with stronger morning light signaling stable circadian rhythm.
Reduced anticipatory worry aligned with stabilized wake cycle.
Lower nocturnal arousal when circadian entrainment improves.
Earlier/more stable sleep onset via daytime circadian cues.
Smoothed morning irritability through improved circadian alignment.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: No sensitive data; ensure privacy of sensor data and consent for wearables.

feasibility.notes: Passive light data is feasible with standard devices; flow to EMAs remains essential for subjective states.

predictors.label: Evening blue-light exposure reduction
predictors.query_text_used: Evening blue-light exposure reduction | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Reducing blue-light exposure in the evening supports melatonin onset and reduces cognitive arousal at night. | Intervention examples: Limit screen use after 9 PM; enable blue-light filter; use warmer lighting. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | Evening screen-time with blue-light exposure minutes | Prior ontology hint: Sleep_circadian_and_restoration / Circadian_Entrains / Light_Cues

mapping.rationale: Variable P02 maps to 'Evening blue-light exposure reduction' and the best-matching predictor leaf is blue_light_reduction_evening (idx 4677). It sits in Sleep circadian and restoration -> light cues -> circadian environment with a specific blue_light_reduction_evening construct, aligning with reducing evening blue-light exposure. The variable's measurement (Evening screen-time with blue-light exposure minutes) is consistent with a blue-light reduction predictor as measured by passive signals. Mechanism rationale matches: reducing evening blue light supports melatonin onset and reduces nocturnal cognitive arousal, improving circadian entrainment and downstream morning reactivity. The pseudoprofile context emphasizes circadian regulation through evening light management, supporting this mapping as the most specific operational match.

source_variable.label: Evening blue-light exposure reduction
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Reducing blue-light exposure in the evening supports melatonin onset and reduces cognitive arousal at night.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Limit screen use after 9 PM; enable blue-light filter; use warmer lighting.

measurement.item_or_signal: Evening screen-time with blue-light exposure minutes
measurement.notes: Captures evening light exposure and device-use habits.

expected_effects.rationale: Evening light reduction may lower nocturnal cognitive arousal.
Less late-night worry due to calmer evenings.
Overall nighttime activation reduced by circadian stabilization.
Improved sleep onset via reduced stimulation.
Less morning irritability through calmer evenings.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Uses passive signals; ensure privacy and opt-in for screen-light data.

feasibility.notes: Practical with most wearables; verify consent for screen-behavior data.

predictors.label: Presleep detachment/unloading routine
predictors.query_text_used: Presleep detachment/unloading routine | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Structured detachment reduces perseverative cognition at sleep onset. | Intervention examples: Detachment routines (e.g., cognitive diffusion, labeling thoughts) before bed. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | In the past 60 minutes, rate how well you detached/unloaded thoughts before sleep (1-9). | Prior ontology hint: Sleep_related_Cognitive_Behavioral_Therapies / Presleep Detachment

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf 3822: PSYCHO / cognitive_behavioral_therapies / sleep_related_cognitive_behavioral_interventions / presleep_mental_skills / detachment_and_unloading / presleep_detachment_routine_design_psychological. This precisely maps to the variable ontology_path Sleep_related_Cognitive_Behavioral_Therapies / Presleep Detachment. The measurement item (past 60 minutes, 1-9) and EMA_self_report modality align with the variable's measurement.mode (EMA_self_report) and measurement.item_or_signal (detached/unloaded thoughts). Mechanistically, the leaf represents a presleep detachment routine designed to reduce perseverative cognition at sleep onset, matching the stated mechanism_rationale. In the pseudoprofile context, P03 is a presleep cognitive strategy; this leaf corresponds to presleep mental skills around detachment/unloading, fitting the intended predictor category.

source_variable.label: Presleep detachment/unloading routine
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Structured detachment reduces perseverative cognition at sleep onset.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Detachment routines (e.g., cognitive diffusion, labeling thoughts) before bed.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 60 minutes, rate how well you detached/unloaded thoughts before sleep (1-9).
measurement.notes: Represents cognitive offloading quality prior to sleep.

expected_effects.rationale: Detachment reduces nocturnal replay by offloading cognitive load.
Less worry propagation into the night when detachment is practiced.
Lower global arousal through cognitive offloading.
Easier sleep initiation via calmer pre-sleep state.
Improved morning mood by reducing pre-sleep arousal.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: No sensitive data; ensure privacy in EMA responses.

feasibility.notes: EMA-based detachment task is feasible with brief prompts.

predictors.label: Presleep cognitive worry reduction (worry writing/diffusion)
predictors.query_text_used: Presleep cognitive worry reduction (worry writing/diffusion) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Presleep cognitive work reduces rumination and improves sleep onset latency. | Intervention examples: Structured worry-diffusion journaling or cognitive diffusion techniques. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | In the past 60 minutes, rate how helpful your presleep worry-diffusion technique was (1-9). | Prior ontology hint: Sleep_related_Cognitive_Behavioral_Therapies / Presleep Worry Diffusion

mapping.rationale: Best match for P04 (Presleep cognitive worry reduction) is the leaf labeled sleep_related_worry_defusion (idx 3824) under Sleep_related_Cognitive_Behavioral_Therapies. This aligns with presleep worry-diffusion techniques, the measurement item (rated usefulness of presleep worry-diffusion in the past 60 minutes), and the mechanism (reducing ruminative thoughts to facilitate sleep onset). It is the most specific operational construct among candidates that captures presleep worry diffusion as a cognitive-behavioral sleep intervention.

source_variable.label: Presleep cognitive worry reduction (worry writing/diffusion)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Presleep cognitive work reduces rumination and improves sleep onset latency.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Structured worry-diffusion journaling or cognitive diffusion techniques.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the past 60 minutes, rate how helpful your presleep worry-diffusion technique was (1-9).
measurement.notes: Captures perceived usefulness of worry-management practice.

expected_effects.rationale: Better worry diffusion reduces nocturnal replay.
Lower anticipatory thoughts at night via worry management.
Decreased nocturnal cognitive arousal through cognitive offloading.
Improved sleep onset via calmer pre-sleep cognitive state.
Less morning irritability secondary to reduced pre-sleep rumination.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Standard EMA data collection; maintain confidentiality of journaling content.

feasibility.notes: Journaling prompts are brief; ensure optional skip options.

predictors.label: Morning routine consistency with light exposure and gentle activity
predictors.query_text_used: Morning routine consistency with light exposure and gentle activity | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Consistent morning cues (light, mild activity) promote stable circadian signaling and improved daytime reactivity. | Intervention examples: Daily outdoor light exposure, light morning walks, gentle mobility. | Measurement: wearable_sensor | Morning_bright_light_minutes_within_2_hours_of_waking | Prior ontology hint: Sleep_circadian_and_restoration / Morning_Routine_Priming

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf maps to morning light exposure as a circadian entrainment cue: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / circadian_entrainment / light_cues / morning_bright_light_exposure. The variable P05 specifies morning routine consistency with light exposure and gentle activity; measurement is Morning_bright_light_minutes_within_2_hours_of_waking collected via smartwatch as a passive daily aggregate, i.e., minutes of bright light after waking. This leaf captures the core mechanism (morning light as circadian anchor) and the exact measurement item. It supports C05 by anchoring mornings to a consistent light cue, aligning with the pseudoprofile's emphasis on morning circadian stabilization.

source_variable.label: Morning routine consistency with light exposure and gentle activity
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Consistent morning cues (light, mild activity) promote stable circadian signaling and improved daytime reactivity.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Daily outdoor light exposure, light morning walks, gentle mobility.

measurement.item_or_signal: Morning_bright_light_minutes_within_2_hours_of_waking
measurement.notes: Aligns with morning circadian anchoring; supports C05 via stable mornings.

expected_effects.rationale: Morning light supports circadian alignment, reducing nighttime cognitive load.
Smoother transitions to night by stabilizing wake-state.
Lower overall arousal with consistent mornings.
Earlier sleep onset from stronger morning cues.
Calmer mornings subsequently reduce irritability.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Uses passive wearables; ensure consent and privacy.

feasibility.notes: Wearable-based morning light data is feasible with standard devices.
